import MethodEndpoint from "@theme/ApiExplorer/MethodEndpoint"

import RequestSchema from "@theme/RequestSchema"

import Heading from "@theme/Heading"
import Translate from "@docusaurus/Translate"

<Heading as={"h1"} className={"openapi__heading"} children={"Accept OAuth 2.0 Login Request"}></Heading>

<MethodEndpoint method={"put"} path={"/admin/oauth2/auth/requests/login/accept"} context={"endpoint"}></MethodEndpoint>

When an authorization code, hybrid, or implicit OAuth 2.0 Flow is initiated, Ory asks the login provider to authenticate the
subject and then tell the Ory OAuth2 Service about it.

The authentication challenge is appended to the login provider URL to which the subject's user-agent (browser) is redirected to.
The login provider uses that challenge to fetch information on the OAuth2 request and then accept or reject the requested
authentication process.

This endpoint tells Ory that the subject has successfully authenticated and includes additional information such as the subject's
ID and if Ory should remember the subject's subject agent for future authentication attempts by setting a cookie.

The response contains a redirect URL which the login provider should redirect the user-agent to.

<Heading id={"request"} as={"h2"} className={"openapi-tabs__heading"}>
  <Translate id="theme.openapi.request.title">Request</Translate>
</Heading>
